title: VoIP Jitter/Latency/Packet Loss (Cisco CUCM)
dataSources: CMR records (jitter, latency, packet loss), RTCP reports
spl:
index=voip sourcetype="cisco:ucm:cmr"
| where jitter > 30 OR latency > 150 OR packet_loss_pct > 1
| stats count by origDeviceName, destDeviceName, jitter, latency, packet_loss_pct
description: Transport quality metrics identify network issues affecting voice quality before users report problems.
value: Transport quality metrics identify network issues affecting voice quality before users report problems.
grandmaExplanation: Transport quality metrics identify network issues affecting voice quality before users report problems — so phishing, data leaks, and account misuse do not slip through.
implementation: Parse transport quality metrics from CMR. Alert on jitter >30ms, latency >150ms, or packet loss >1%. Correlate with WAN/LAN performance metrics. Track per-site to identify network segments needing attention.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: UCM CMR, RTCP data.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CMR records (jitter, latency, packet loss), RTCP reports.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Parse transport quality metrics from CMR. Alert on jitter >30ms, latency >150ms, or packet loss >1%. Correlate with WAN/LAN performance metrics. Track per-site to identify network segments needing attention.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=voip sourcetype="cisco:ucm:cmr"
| where jitter > 30 OR latency > 150 OR packet_loss_pct > 1
| stats count by origDeviceName, destDeviceName, jitter, latency, packet_loss_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**VoIP Jitter/Latency/Packet Loss (Cisco CUCM)** — Transport quality metrics identify network issues affecting voice quality before users report problems.

Documented **Data sources**: CMR records (jitter, latency, packet loss), RTCP reports. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): UCM CMR, RTCP data. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: voip; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucm:cmr. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=voip, sourcetype="cisco:ucm:cmr". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where jitter > 30 OR latency > 150 OR packet_loss_pct > 1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by origDeviceName, destDeviceName, jitter, latency, packet_loss_pct** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Multi-metric chart (jitter, latency, packet loss), Table (calls with poor transport), Heatmap (site × metric).
visualization: Multi-metric chart (jitter, latency, packet loss), Table (calls with poor transport), Heatmap (site × metric).
hardware: Cisco Unified Communications Manager (CUCM), Unity Connection, IP Phone 7800 series, IP Phone 8800 series
